title: Multiple Dates on Single Row Gantt View
excerpt:

posts.author: mitchellgp (Mitchell)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is it possible to have more than one date per row on the gantt view?

We have a column for a product’s due date and then a seperate column for its release date. We’d previously had both dates visible on our manual gantt calendar we had on google sheets but there doesn’t seem to be a nice way of doing this on monday.com.

When selecting more than one date, it creates a new row, we want to have the option to join the dates to the products ONE row on the gantt view. And also have it clearly labelled rather than using just the items name for both dates. In an ideal world, colour them differently! Green for it’s release date and orange for the due date all on one row, what a dream…

posts.author: meganmansfield (Megan Mansfield)
posts.cookedHtml:
@BiancaT any thoughts on this? To my knowledge, the gantt’s are unable to do this. However, it would be very helpful to be able to gather the various date columns into on gantt.
For example: I have the below board and would love it if I could get the various dates on a gantt sorted by “Task” or in this case community.

image1121×207 21.6 KB

posts.author: cdesign (Claire)
posts.cookedHtml: Has anyone learned where this may be on Monday’s product road map? It makes it so difficult for our team to use Monday for Gantt Charts that we’ve stopped trying, and instead have gone back to other software to solve the problem. The Gantt Charts are just too long and lengthy to make sense or be usable, when each date must have its own line item.
